title: Additional distribution-channel adapter (2nd host)
context: After the first publish adapter (adapter-003) is operational, a second distribution channel adapter reduces single-channel dependency and broadens the reach of published read-alongs. This requires a second partner to be secured (per research-001's decision tree) and their format, license, and AI-audio stance to be confirmed. This is a backlog item that becomes actionable only after M1 is complete and a second channel is secured.
objective: Implement a second publish adapter in adapters/publish-<channel2>/ for a newly confirmed distribution channel, following the same neutral adapter interface as adapter-003.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements a publish adapter for a second confirmed distribution channel behind the same neutral adapters/publish interface used by adapter-003.
Submits the full read-along package with license, attribution, consent reference, provenance, and human/synthetic label per the second channel's requirements.
Documents the second channel's acceptance posture, format requirements, AI-audio stance, and required output license.
Honors the second channel's rate limits and API/contribution policies; never writes secrets to logs or committed files.
Passes pnpm build and pnpm test; pnpm lint clean.
Licensed MIT; DCO sign-off on all commits.
resources: read-aloud-audio-adapter-003 (first publish adapter, neutral interface reference)
output: A merged PR adding adapters/publish-<channel2>/ with the second-channel publish adapter implementation, unit tests, and documentation covering the channel-specific mechanism, package requirements, and secrets handling.
